title: 200 Objects Infants EEG
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from human infants viewing 200 object images presented in rapid visual streams. The study investigates neural responses to visual object categories in developing brains, providing insights into early visual processing and object recognition mechanisms in infancy. Raw EEG data are accompanied by preprocessing scripts and representational similarity analysis code for reproducible analysis.
methods_description: EEG data were collected while infants viewed 200 object images presented in rapid visual streams. Stimulus presentation was controlled using PsychoPy (version 2023.3.1). Data preprocessing, epoching, and analysis were performed using MATLAB-based toolboxes (FieldTrip and CoSMoMVPA), including representational similarity analysis (RSA) to examine neural representations of object categories.
